text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Everwood, the statue of Lady Fortune looked a bit bored. She'd sit all day on her hillside, watching the villagers go about their business. One lazy afternoon, Jack Frost decided to give her a frosty makeover.

He chipped away some of the rust, revealing streaks of icy blue. Then he giggled. "You want some personality?"

Without warning, Lady Fortune's new icy persona sparkled. She leaped and pirouetted. "Oh dear! How delightful!" she exclaimed before she melted away into snowflakes.

From that day on, Lady Fortune roamed Everwood in whimsical icicles. She'd dance around on icy ponds, serenading ice dragons.

Kids loved her. She'd give them treasures made out of flakes. Her reputation spread.

One chilly Christmas Eve, a young girl named Olive climbed up to look down on her.

"How did you do that?" Olive wondered.
